skill.name: elite-mvp-master
skill.description: A development guidance skill for building OC Pipeline features with production-ready practices across React, Node.js, Supabase, and Vercel.
skill.summary: Guides production-ready MVP development for OC Pipeline and related construction software.
skill.author: ALPA-Const

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm on SKILL.md line 3. Manual review found this is a false positive in descriptive YAML metadata, with no cryptographic code, execution path, or data handling behavior present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is a YAML description for a coding guidance skill. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, executable code, commands, network behavior, or secret handling in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The finding has very low confidence because the flagged text is plain metadata, not a crypto API or executable instruction. The reviewed file contains no supporting evidence of weak cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: Nontechnical founders and project owners need complete software changes without vague developer instructions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to produce production-ready code and clear implementation steps.
actual_capabilities: Applies production-ready development standards to feature, bug fix, and component requests.
Guides work involving React, Node.js, Supabase, Vercel, databases, and APIs.
Emphasizes security, scalability, maintainability, error handling, and edge cases.
Requests complete working files instead of partial snippets or placeholders.
Formats instructions as direct numbered steps for nontechnical users.
limitations: The skill is guidance-only and does not include executable scripts or reusable code assets.
It is tailored to OC Pipeline and may need adaptation for unrelated products.
It depends on the active model having access to the relevant project files.
It does not replace formal security, SOC 2, or NIST compliance review.

use_cases.title: Ship SaaS Features
use_cases.description: Turn a plain-language feature request into complete implementation guidance for a construction management SaaS product.

use_cases.title: Fix Production Bugs
use_cases.description: Ask the assistant to diagnose a visible issue and return complete corrected files with direct implementation steps.

use_cases.title: Review MVP Quality
use_cases.description: Apply security, maintainability, and error-handling standards before deploying a new MVP workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Simple Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new product capability.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use elite-mvp-master to build this feature for OC Pipeline: [describe the feature]. Give complete files and simple numbered steps.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Bug
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a broken screen or workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use elite-mvp-master to fix this bug: [describe what is broken and what should happen]. Include complete working code and exact steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Database Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request involving Supabase or API behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use elite-mvp-master to design and implement this Supabase workflow: [describe data, users, and actions]. Include validation and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Release
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before deploying a production change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use elite-mvp-master to review this planned release for security, scalability, maintainability, and missing edge cases: [describe release].

output_examples.input: Build a project dashboard for OC Pipeline users.
output: States the dashboard feature being built in one sentence.
Provides complete files for the React page, data loading, and error states.
Gives numbered steps for where to place files and how to test the screen.

output_examples.input: Fix a Supabase save error on a construction estimate form.
output: Explains the bug fix in plain language.
Returns complete corrected files with validation and user-facing error handling.
Lists exact deployment and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Review a new API endpoint before production release.
output: Identifies missing security checks and edge cases.
Suggests production-ready changes for request validation and failure handling.
Provides a short release checklist for the project owner.

best_practices: Provide the current file paths, visible error messages, and expected behavior before asking for a fix.
Ask for complete files when the change touches shared components, APIs, or database logic.
Request a deployment and verification checklist for every production change.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to bypass formal compliance or security approvals.
Do not ask for placeholder code when the change will be used in production.
Do not apply generated changes without reviewing project-specific requirements and tests.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides software development work for OC Pipeline, including features, bug fixes, components, APIs, and database changes.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it run code or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains guidance text only and no executable scripts.

faq.question: Is it only for construction software?
faq.answer: It is written for OC Pipeline and construction SaaS, but some development standards may apply to other software projects.

faq.question: Can it replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No. It encourages secure development practices, but it does not replace a formal security or compliance review.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: A static warning was reviewed and dismissed as a false positive, but the report keeps a low-risk note for transparency.
